bids.reasoning: This is the opening at t=0.0 with no opponent offers or messages to infer their preferences, so I am targeting my maximum-utility bid. With no time pressure yet, I should anchor strongly on my best values across all issues.

I am targeting a very high-utility bid because it is still the opening phase at t=0.02, so there is little reason to make major concessions yet. The opponent’s bid is the worst valid package for me and their message is only an emotional appeal, not evidence of real concession, so I will make only a small move on my lowest-weight issue while holding firm on the important issues.

At t=0.04 it is still very early, so I am targeting a very high-utility bid while making a small concession on my lowest-weight issue. The opponent has repeated the same all-valueA offer, which gives me very low utility, and their messages are emotional appeals rather than evidence of real concession, so I should not move much yet.

I am targeting a very high-utility bid because it is still very early at t=0.06, and the opponent has only made a minimal numerical concession so far. Their messages emphasize partnership, but the actual offers remain strongly opposed to my preferences, with only issueD moving from valueA to valueB. I will concede further on my lowest-weight issueA while holding firm on the higher-weight issues, especially issueD and issueC.

I am targeting a high utility bid around 0.935 because it is still very early at t=0.08 and the opponent’s offers remain very low for me, improving only slightly on issueD. Their messages emphasize partnership, but the actual bids show minimal concession, so I will continue conceding mainly on my least important issueA while holding firm on higher-weight issues B, C, and D.

At t=0.10 it is still early, so I am targeting a high-utility bid while continuing to make visible concessions on my lowest-weight issue. The opponent has only moved slightly from all valueA offers, and their messages are emotional appeals rather than evidence of real constraints, so I will not concede on high-weight issueC or issueD yet. This bid gives me about 0.92 utility and avoids repeating my last exact offer.

I am targeting a high-utility bid around 0.888, since it is still early at t=0.12 and the opponent’s offers remain very low for me despite small concessions. The opponent has only moved slightly on issueC/issueD while repeatedly asking for partnership, so I treat the messages as cheap talk and concede mainly on lower-weight issues while protecting issueC and issueD.

I am targeting a high-utility bid around 0.856, since it is still early at t=0.14 and there is no need to make large concessions yet. The opponent’s offers remain very low for my utility, with only small movement mainly on issueD and occasional issueC, while their messages are emotional but not backed by substantial concessions. I will concede further on lower-weight issueB while holding firm on the more important issueC and issueD.

I am still early in the negotiation at t=0.16, so I am targeting a high-utility bid for me while continuing a measured concession path; this offer gives me about 0.824 utility. The opponent’s offers remain very low for me, with only small scattered concessions, and their messages are mostly emotional appeals rather than reliable commitments. I will concede further on the lower-weight issueB while holding firm on the high-weight issueC and issueD.

I am targeting a still-strong utility around 0.79 because it is early in the negotiation and the opponent’s offers remain very low for me despite small, inconsistent movements. Since I have already conceded fully on issueA and issueB is less important to me than issueC and issueD, I will concede further on issueB while holding firm on the high-weight issues.

I am targeting a still-strong utility around 0.76 by finishing concessions on the lowest-weight issues while preserving issueC and issueD, which are most important to me. At t=0.20 there is still time, and the opponent’s actual bids remain very low for me despite small movements, so I should not concede on high-weight issues yet. Their messages emphasize partnership, but the numbers show only limited concession, so I will move on issueB while holding issueC and issueD firm.

I am targeting a utility around 0.71, which is a meaningful concession from my prior 0.76 while still protecting my highest-weight issueD. At t=0.22 there is still time, so I should not collapse toward the opponent’s low offers; their bids show only limited concessions, mostly around issueC/issueD, and their partnership language is cheap talk rather than binding evidence.

I am targeting a still-strong utility around 0.66 by conceding further on issueC while protecting issueD, my highest-weight issue. At t=0.24 there is still time, so I should not collapse to the opponent’s low offers; their bids show only limited movement, with the latest concession mainly on issueC, while their partnership language is non-binding cheap talk.

I am targeting a still-strong utility around 0.616, conceding further on issueC while holding issueD at my top value because it has the highest weight for me. At t=0.26 there is some time pressure but not enough to drop near the opponent’s very low offers; their messages are cooperative but the actual bids show only slow, limited concessions and remain far below my prior offers.

I am targeting about 0.60 utility, since we are still in the early-middle phase and the opponent’s offers remain quite low for me despite some movement. The opponent has repeatedly signaled willingness to move a little on issueC and issueD, while their messages are mostly partnership appeals rather than binding constraints, so I will make a controlled concession on my highest issueD only one step while keeping value on issueC.

I am targeting a moderate concession around 0.57 utility: lower than my last offer but still preserving value on the most important issues, especially issueD and issueC. At t=0.30 there is still time, so I should not drop to the opponent’s very low offers yet; their bidding shows only slow concessions despite cooperative/emotional messages, so I treat the messages as cheap talk and respond with a concrete tradeoff.

I am targeting a moderate utility around 0.55 since we are still before the midpoint but the opponent has started offering better terms, especially on issueD. Their messages are cooperative but mostly emotional cheap talk, so I am relying on the offer pattern: they have signaled willingness to accept issueD: valueE while still pressing hard for low values elsewhere. I will concede on the lower-weight issues A and B and partially on C, while preserving strong value on the highest-weight issue D.

I am targeting a moderate utility around 0.584, which is still safely above reservation while making a meaningful mid-game concession on issueD compared with holding at valueE/valueF. The opponent’s latest bid shows willingness to move substantially on issueC but continued resistance on issueD, so I am accepting their movement on issueC while asking for a less extreme concession on the high-weight issueD; their partnership messages are noted but not treated as binding evidence.

I am targeting a moderate mid-game utility around 0.55 while still protecting issueD, my highest-weight issue. The opponent’s offers remain low for my utility and their messages are mostly emotional appeals, but they have occasionally signaled willingness to move on issueD, so I am trading down on issueC while keeping issueD relatively favorable. Since we are only at t=0.36, I will concede but not drop near their latest low-utility proposal.
